title: Lowercase symlink strategy for mixed-case path components
description:
When repos are checked out into the archive, path components may contain mixed case (e.g. foo/BAR/baz/bunk). This makes tab-completion and filesystem navigation harder on case-sensitive systems.

Add a symlink strategy that creates lowercase symlinks for any path component that is mixed-case. For example, foo/BAR/baz/bunk would get a foo/bar → foo/BAR symlink created (if foo/bar doesn't already exist).

This should be a strategy that runs after sync completes, not blocking the main sync path. It should be idempotent and skip if the lowercase path already exists (as a real dir or symlink).
acceptance_criteria:
- After archive sync, mixed-case path components get lowercase symlinks
- foo/BAR/baz → foo/bar symlink pointing to foo/BAR
- Idempotent: skips if lowercase path already exists
- Does not block or fail the main sync
- Configurable (opt-in or opt-out)

title: Priority-based repo naming strategies for multi-host collision avoidance
description:
Archive and wiki path construction (src/archive/sync.ts, src/wiki/sync.ts) uses url.pathname alone, dropping the host. This means github.com/org/repo and gitlab.com/org/repo both map to ~/archive/org/repo — a collision.

The single-segment fix (using host as org) helps root-level repos, but multi-segment paths on different forges still collide.

Design a priority-based strategy system for deriving archive paths:

- **Priority 0 (default)**: current pathname-based strategy — org/repo from URL path
- **Higher priority**: strategies that include host, forge-specific conventions, or user-configured overrides
- Strategies compose: highest-priority match wins, falling back to 0-priority default

Evaluate how rolldown (the bundler) implements its filtering and transform pipeline as a reference for how to compose ordered strategies with overrides. Specifically look at how rolldown allows plugins to register transforms with relative priority and how filtering chains compose.

This needs a design phase before implementation — the strategy interface, priority ordering, and how strategies are registered (per-provider? global config?) all need decisions.
acceptance_criteria:
- ADR or design doc for priority-based repo naming strategies
- Current pathname-based naming is the 0-priority default
- Higher-priority strategies can override (e.g. host-qualified paths, forge-specific conventions)
- Evaluate rolldown's filtering/transform pipeline as a reference for composing strategies
- No regressions to existing archive path mappings (or a migration plan)

title: Watch mode retry and recovery
description:
Watch mode (src/dl/watch.ts) has no retry or recovery. If a queued entry fails, hadError accumulates but processing continues. A persistent failure (e.g. a dead forge URL in archlist) will spam errors forever with no backoff or skip mechanism.

Add retry with exponential backoff for failed entries, with a configurable max retry count after which the entry is skipped and logged as permanently failed.
acceptance_criteria:
- Failed entries have configurable retry with exponential backoff
- Persistent failures eventually skip (with a configurable max retries)
- Watch mode logs degraded entries distinctly from transient failures
- No infinite error spam for dead forges

title: Consolidate parseArgs into gunshi define
description:
src/dl/args.ts has a hand-rolled parseArgs function that duplicates the arg definitions already declared in the gunshi define() block at src/command/dl.ts:156-203. Having two separate arg definitions will drift over time — defaults, flag names, and types can silently diverge.

Consolidate so arg definitions live in one place. The gunshi define block should be the source of truth; parseArgs should either be removed or reduced to extracting positional inputs from the already-parsed gunshi result.
acceptance_criteria:
- src/dl/args.ts parseArgs is removed or reduced to a thin shim
- Arg definitions live in one place (the gunshi define block)
- No duplicated arg names, types, or defaults between two locations
- Existing CLI behavior preserved

title: No timeouts on git sync operations
description:
The resolve phase has RESOLVE_TIMEOUT (8s) but the actual git sync operations (cloneOrUpdate, ensureJjInitialized in git ops) have no signal or timeout. A hung git server blocks the process forever.

Add timeout/abort support to all git subprocess invocations.
acceptance_criteria:
- All git subprocess invocations (clone, pull, fetch, jj init) accept an AbortSignal or timeout
- Default timeout is reasonable (e.g. 60s for clone, 30s for pull)
- Hung git servers don't block the process indefinitely
- Existing tests pass

title: Redirect handling ADR and implementation
description:
The dl pipeline currently does no redirect handling. urlExists uses redirect: "manual" and only checks status code range. When a forge redirects (e.g. tangled.sh → tangled.org, or a forge redirecting HTTP→HTTPS or www→bare), the original URL is kept rather than the canonical target.

Write an ADR deciding:
- Where redirect resolution happens (during expansion? during provider resolve? as a separate middleware stage?)
- Whether to always follow redirects or only in specific providers
- How to handle redirect chains and limits
- Whether to store both original and canonical URLs in RepoContext

Then implement the ADR decision.
acceptance_criteria:
- ADR document written and reviewed before implementation
- Redirects are resolved at a defined pipeline stage
- Resolved (canonical) URLs propagate through to syncArchive/syncWiki
- Generic provider benefits from redirect resolution for forges that redirect repo URLs
- Existing tests pass

title: Precise registry matching with host canonicalization ADR
description:
Current registry lookup (src/repo/registry.ts:22-24) uses host.includes(pattern) substring matching. This means not-github.com would match github.com — a false positive.

Beyond fixing the substring match, we need to handle host canonicalization. For example www.github.com should resolve to the github provider. This needs an ADR to decide where in the pipeline canonicalization occurs.

Tentative options for the ADR to evaluate:

**A: Canonicalize in expanders** — expanders normalize www.github.com → github.com before producing candidate URLs. Simple but each expander needs awareness.

**B: Canonicalize in registry lookup** — registry.lookup() strips known prefixes before matching. Centralized but registry needs a canonicalization mapping.

**C: Canonicalize middleware stage** — new pipeline stage between expansion and registry that normalizes URLs. Clean separation but adds complexity.

**D: Provider-driven canonicalization** — each provider declares all host variants at registration time. Most explicit, no runtime normalization needed.

The ADR should evaluate these and select one. Implementation follows the ADR decision.
acceptance_criteria:
- ADR document written and reviewed before implementation
- Registry lookup no longer produces false positives from substring matching
- Canonicalization strategy for www-prefixed and other common host variants is documented and implemented
- The ADR covers where in the pipeline canonicalization occurs (expander stage vs registry stage vs both)
- Existing tests pass, new tests for canonicalization and precise matching

title: Fallthrough strategy list for generic provider URL verification
description:
The generic provider (src/repo/provider/generic.ts) currently does a single HEAD request to verify a URL exists. This is unreliable — many git forges return 404 on HEAD to the repo root even when the repo is valid.

Design a fallthrough strategy system that tries multiple verification strategies in order, stopping at the first success:

1. HEAD request (current behavior, fast but loose)
2. GET with range header or similar to get confirmable bytes early
3. git ls-remote (definitive but heavier — spawns a subprocess)

Key requirement: early-cancel. Once a strategy starts writing confirmable bytes back (e.g. git ls-remote starts streaming refs), we should be able to cancel and proceed, rather than waiting for the full response.

The strategy list should be configurable per-provider if needed, but the generic provider should have a sensible default chain.
acceptance_criteria:
- Generic provider uses a strategy chain instead of single HEAD
- git ls-remote is the fallback strategy
- Early-cancel works: strategy can signal success as soon as confirmable bytes arrive
- Existing tests still pass
- New tests cover each strategy independently and the fallthrough ordering

title: Rekon dl pipeline robustness
description:
The dl pipeline has a clean layered architecture (Input → Expanders → Registry → Provider.resolve → enrich → syncArchive/syncWiki) but multiple fragilities were identified during review of url handling for non-github forges like git.ffmpeg.org and tangled.org.

Key concern areas:
- Generic provider relies on HEAD requests which are unreliable for many git forges
- Registry lookup uses substring matching that can misroute hosts
- No redirect handling means canonical URLs are lost
- No timeouts on git operations (clone, pull, jj init)
- Hand-rolled parseArgs duplicates gunshi arg definitions
- Watch mode has no retry/backoff for persistent failures
- Path construction drops host info, causing collisions across forges

These all need addressing to make dl reliable across the diversity of git hosting setups in the wild.

title: rekon-dl-tangled
description:
Add support for tangled.org as a source domain in the dl command.

Tangled.org is a code collaboration platform. The dl command should be able to:
- Parse tangled.org repository URLs
- Clone/fetch repositories from tangled.org
- Support wiki downloads from tangled.org if available

This requires understanding tangled.org's URL structure and API/git access patterns.

title: Add archive wiki and archlist Gunshi plugins
Split dl process into archive and wiki domain modules
Add roots and repo Gunshi plugins
Add decomposition tests and extension docs
Normalize command wiring to thin orchestration
dl --watch queue enhancements
description:
Enhancements to the dl --watch command for better archlist queue processing.

Includes:
- Short name expansion in archlist entries
- Parallel wiki/archive downloads
- Skip markers for archlist entries

title: Add skip marker for archlist entries
description:
Add a way to mark entries in ~/archlist that should be skipped during queue processing.

Possible implementations:
- Comment prefix: Lines starting with '#' are ignored
- Inline comment: 'rektide/myrepo # skip this' ignores everything after '#'
- Negation prefix: Lines starting with '!' or '-' are skipped

This allows users to:
- Temporarily disable repos without deleting them from the list
- Add notes/documentation to the archlist
- Keep a wishlist of repos to maybe process later

title: Decouple wiki and archive downloads in dl command
description:
Make wiki and archive downloads run concurrently instead of sequentially in the dl command's processInput function.

Currently (lines 425-491 in dl.ts):
1. Archive download completes
2. Then wiki download starts

With this change:
- Both downloads start in parallel
- Each proceeds as fast as possible
- Linking step still waits for both to complete

This will significantly speed up processing when both --archive and --wiki are enabled.

title: Expand short names in archlist during queue processing
description:
When processing entries from ~/archlist in --watch mode, expand short repository names (e.g., 'rektide/myrepo') to their full clone URLs before processing. This allows users to append short-form names to archlist and have them resolved to full URLs as they're consumed.

Currently archlist entries are processed as-is. With this change:
- Append 'rektide/myrepo' to archlist
- Queue processor resolves to 'https://github.com/rektide/myrepo.git'
- Processing continues with the resolved URL

title: Add symlink transforms for alternate case/punctuation
description:
Add capability to create symlinks with alternate transformations of project names.

Example use cases:
- Lowercase: `rektide/MyProject` → symlink `rektide/myproject`
- Remove punctuation: `rektide/my-project` → symlink `rektide/myproject`
- Combined: `rektide/My-Project` → symlink `rektide/myproject`

This helps with discoverability when project names have different casing or punctuation in different contexts (e.g., GitHub uses lowercase in URLs but repos may have mixed case).

title: Add ETag-based conditional fetching for repository scraper
description:
Implement ETag checking in the scraper to avoid unnecessary re-downloads. When fetching resources (wiki pages, releases, etc.), store ETags and use If-None-Match headers on subsequent requests to only download changed content.

This will:
- Reduce bandwidth usage
- Speed up repeated sync operations
- Be kinder to remote servers

title: Add --no-log-cache flag to dl to disable logging of cached file names
description: Add a `--no-log-cache` flag to the `dl` command that disables logging of cached file names. When this flag is set, the tool should not output/log the names of files that were served from cache.

title: Add interpolation-execution mode to interpolate command
description:
After the interpolate command has been created, add an execution mode that can run the interpolated result as a shell command. This would allow users to interpolate variables into command templates and then execute them, similar to how opencode handles commands with interpolated arguments.

Example usage:
```bash
rekon interpolate -t 'echo "Hello $1"' world --execute
# Would output: Hello world

rekon interpolate -t 'ls -la $1' /tmp --execute
# Would run: ls -la /tmp
```

This would be useful for:
- Testing command templates with variable arguments
- Executing commands with variable substitutions without full opencode
- Creating reusable command templates with positional parameters
- Testing prompt/ file templates before using them with opencode

title: Create install-commands subcommand
description:
Implement `install-commands` gunshi subcommand that:
- Scans all markdown/mdx files in prompt/ recursively
- Creates symlinks in ~/.config/opencode/command/
- Checks symlink status with readlink before creating
- Reports per-file status: "already-done", "done", "error-existing-file"
- Supports -f flag to force overwrite existing files
- Creates directory if needed
- Uses async/promises (node:fs/promises) not sync operations
acceptance_criteria:
- install-commands subcommand exists in command/
- Finds all .md and .mdx files in prompt/ recursively
- Creates symlinks to ~/.config/opencode/command/
- Reports status per file: "already-done", "done", "error-existing-file"
- -f flag forces overwrite of existing files
- Uses async/promises from node:fs/promises
- Passes typecheck and lint
- Files are executable with shebang

title: Create rekon.ts as gunshi main program with combine subcommand
description: Create rekon.ts in top-level directory as gunshi main program, move combine logic to command/combine.ts as a subcommand, update rekon.ts to register combine command

title: Implement file validation and glob pattern matching
description: Search prompt/ directory for matching files using glob patterns, filter out files that don't exist or can't be read, handle both explicit filenames and glob patterns in arguments

title: Create combine.sh script to merge prompt plans
description: Build a Node.js CLI tool using gunshi that combines all markdown plans from prompt/ directory into a single markdown file. The tool should use file patterns or filenames as arguments, search for valid files, and add headers to plans that don't start with '#'.

title: Check for front-matter description in command files
description: When installing commands, check front-matter and warn if description field is missing. Commands should have descriptions according to opencode documentation.
acceptance_criteria:
- Parse front-matter from markdown files
- Check for description field in front-matter
- Use console.warn if description is missing
- Add -q/--quiet flag to suppress warnings
- Only check front-matter when not in dry-run mode (or maybe always check?)
notes:
Implementation:
- Added gray-matter package for front-matter parsing
- Check for description field in front-matter
- Use console.warn if description is missing
- Added -q/--quiet flag to suppress warnings
- Only check front-matter when scanning files (works in both normal and dry-run mode)
close_reason: Implemented front-matter description checking with warnings. Added -q flag to suppress warnings.

title: Add dry run mode to install-commands
description:
Add -n/--dry-run flag to install-commands that:
- Doesn't actually create or modify any files
- Shows what would happen (same status reporting)
- Useful for previewing changes before running
acceptance_criteria:
- -n flag supported, short and long form
- Does not create or modify any symlinks
- Reports same statuses: "already-done", "done", "error-existing-file"
- Works with -f flag combination (shows what would be force-overwritten)
notes:
Implementation:
- Added -n/--dry-run flag to install-commands
- When dry run is enabled, reports what would happen without making changes
- Works with -f flag combination to show what would be force-overwritten
- Uses xdgConfig from xdg-basedir package for XDG-compliant config directory
- All acceptance criteria met
close_reason: Implemented -n/--dry-run flag that shows what would happen without creating/modifying files. Works with -f flag to show force overwrites.
